911 CALL CENTER REPORTS SIGNIFICANT FLOODING WITHIN THE COMMUNITY OF ARARAT RESULTING IN NUMEROUS ROAD CLOSURES AND TREE DAMAGE. FLOODING IS STILL ONGOING WITH LITTLE TO NO SPECIFIC DETAILS ON SEVERITY AND LOCALITY.
TRAINING SHOWERS AND STORMS RESULTED IN FLASH FLOODING AT THE INTERSECTION OF HOOTIN HOLLER TRAIL AND RADAR ROAD AS A PRIVATE POND OVERFLOWED ITS BANKS.
TRAINING SHOWERS AND STORMS RESULTED IN FLASH FLOODING THAT CAUSED THE ARARAT RIVER TO TOP ITS BANKS FLOODING PORTIONS OF RADAR ROAD.

SURRY COUNTY, NC. EM REPORTS TWO SWIFT WATER RESCUES CONDUCTED ALONG RADAR ROAD AFTER VEHICLES WERE SWEPT INTO THE ARARAT RIVER. NO HUMAN INJURIES REPORTED. EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO THE ROAD DID OCCUR DUE TO THE FLASH FLOODING.

PERSISTENT HEAVY DOWNPOURS OVER THE CENTRAL TO EASTERN PORTIONS OF SURRY COUNTY RESULTED IN SEVERAL RIVERS AND STREAMS TO OVERFLOW THEIR BANKS INUNDATING ROADWAYS. THIS INCLUDES ARARAT RIVER, STEWARTS CREEK, AND LOVILLS CREEK. FLOODING IS STILL ONGOING IN MOST OF THESE BASINS AS OF 930PM EDT.
REPETITIVE HEAVY DOWNPOURS OVER THE CITY OF ARARAT RESULTED IN ONGOING FLASH FLOODING WHICH INUNDATED PORTIONS OF CITY PARK.

PERSISTENT HEAVY RAINFALL RESULTED IN PILOT CREEK OVERFLOWING ITS BANKS ACROSS JIM MCKINNEY ROAD. OBSERVER MEASURED 8 INCHES OF PRECIPITATION IN A 24 HOUR PERIOD BEFORE HIS GAUGE OVERFLOWED. ESTIMATES 10+ INCHES AND CONTINUING.
EXCESSIVE RUNOFF FROM TRAINING DOWNPOURS CAUSED FLASH WATER TO OVERFLOW SMALL STREAMS AND DRAINAGE DITCHES ALONG OLD 52 NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ARMSTRONG ROAD.
